text.title: Gcp Billing Cost Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example for the gcp-billing-cost methodology; demonstrates the full procedure resolving to a valid output artefact.

example.content:
A FinOps team runs the monthly cost report on the platform billing account.
Inputs:
- Period: 2026-04-01 → 2026-04-30. - Projects: 18 across 4 folders.
Output report (JSON):
```json { "period": "2026-04", "projects": ["prod-commerce-api", "prod-ml-training", "nonprod-platform-vpc"], "top_cost_drivers": [ {"service": "Compute Engine", "amount_usd": 12400, "share_pct": 41}, {"service": "BigQuery", "amount_usd": 6800, "share_pct": 22}, {"service": "GKE", "amount_usd": 5400, "share_pct": 18} ], "savings_opportunities": [ {"id": "idle-cloud-run", "estimated_savings_usd": 320}, {"id": "oversized-cloud-sql", "estimated_savings_usd": 1100}, {"id": "cud-eligible-baseline", "estimated_savings_usd": 4200} ], "recommendations": [ "Apply 1y CUD on stable compute baseline (8 vCPU / 32GB)", "Right-size prod-commerce-db from db-custom-8-32 to db-custom-4-16", "Delete 3 idle Cloud Run services" ] } ```
